** Note: This is a duplicate of SNYK-GOLANG-GITHUBCOMCREWJAMSAML-1050998.

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/internal/config/c.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) where the node can be exhausted of memory or disk space if the output of the ExecSync command is large enough, depending on the node the command is being run on. As a result, other services on the node (e.g. other containers) will be unable to allocate memory.

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/internal/oci.remediation: Upgrade github.com/cri-o/cri-o/internal/oci to version 1.22.5, 1.23.3, 1.24.1 or higher.
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/internal/oci.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) where the node can be exhausted of memory or disk space if the output of the ExecSync command is large enough, depending on the node the command is being run on. As a result, other services on the node (e.g. other containers) will be unable to allocate memory.

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pkg/config.remediation: Upgrade github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pkg/config to version 1.22.5, 1.23.3, 1.24.1 or higher.
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pkg/config.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) where the node can be exhausted of memory or disk space if the output of the ExecSync command is large enough, depending on the node the command is being run on. As a result, other services on the node (e.g. other containers) will be unable to allocate memory.

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/internal/factory/.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Default Permissions where containers are incorrectly started with non-empty inheritable Linux process capabilities, creating an atypical Linux environment and enabling programs with inheritable file capabilities to elevate those capabilities to the permitted set during execve(2).

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.remediation: Upgrade github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server to version 1.24.0 or higher.
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.overview:
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server is an OCI-based implementation of Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Default Permissions where containers are incorrectly started with non-empty inheritable Linux process capabilities, creating an atypical Linux environment and enabling programs with inheritable file capabilities to elevate those capabilities to the permitted set during execve(2).

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pkg/config.remediation: Upgrade github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pkg/config to version 1.19.6, 1.20.7, 1.21.6, 1.22.3, 1.23.2 or higher.
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pkg/config.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary Code Execution due to a flaw that makes it possible for an attacker to bypass the safeguards and set arbitrary kernel parameters on the host.
As a result, anyone with rights to deploy a pod on a Kubernetes cluster that uses the CRI-O runtime can abuse the “kernel.core_pattern” kernel parameter to achieve container escape and arbitrary code execution as root on any node in the cluster.

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.remediation: Upgrade github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server to version 1.26.0 or higher.
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.overview:
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server is an OCI-based implementation of Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation such that it is possible to craft an environment variable with newlines to add entries to a container's /etc/passwd. It is possible to circumvent admission validation of username/UID by adding such an entry.

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.remediation: Upgrade github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server to version 1.25.0 or higher.
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.overview:
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server is an OCI-based implementation of Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure due to incorrect handling of the supplementary groups. 
Note: This is exploitable only if the attacker has direct access to the affected container where supplementary groups are used to set access permissions and is able to execute a binary code in that container.

github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.vul_name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.remediation: Upgrade github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server to version 1.24.0 or higher.
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server.overview:
github.com/cri-o/cri-o/server is an OCI-based implementation of Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource. The sysctls from the list of "safe" sysctls specified for the cluster will be applied to the host if an attacker can create a pod with a hostIPC and hostNetwork kernel namespace.

github.com/datacharmer/dbdeployer/unpack.remediation: Upgrade github.com/datacharmer/dbdeployer/unpack to version 1.58.2 or higher.
github.com/datacharmer/dbdeployer/unpack.overview:
github.com/datacharmer/dbdeployer/unpack is a tool that deploys MySQL database servers easily. This is a port of MySQL-Sandbox, originally written in Perl, and re-designed from the ground up in Go. See the features comparison for more detail.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Symlink Attack. When unpacking a maliciously packaged tarball that contains symlinks to files external to the target, an attacker could induce dbdeployer to write into a system file by injecting a symbolic link into the tarball.

go.dedis.ch/kyber/v3.remediation: Upgrade go.dedis.ch/kyber/v3 to version 3.0.13 or higher.
go.dedis.ch/kyber/v3.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cryptographic Issues. If a malicious attacker adds one of points of the subgroups of small order (i.e., non-identity torsion points) to a key image, up to 7 double spends could be performed for each authentic spend on the cothority: this is now prevented by the Ge25519HasSmallOrder function.
This happens because multiplying the sum of a torsion point by a multiple of 8 and a "normal" point will return the same point, because multiplying small subgroup points by 8 returns the identity element. Without the function Ge25519HasSmallOrder, signatures verify on malicious key images derived from the authentic ones.

github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml to version 2.27.0 or higher.
github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Validation. A vulnerability exists in the SAML connector of the github.com/dexidp/dex library used to process SAML Signature Validation. This flaw allows an attacker to bypass SAML authentication. The highest threat from this vulnerability is to confidentiality, integrity, as well as system availability.

github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml to version 2.27.0 or higher.
github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Signature Validation Bypass. Disclosures of a few vulnerabilities impact users leveraging the SAML connector.

github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml to version 2.27.0 or higher.
github.com/dexidp/dex/connector/saml.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. It is affected by 3 CVEs relating to XML attribute instability in encoding/xml dependency. Fix mitigates the vulnerability by validating XML is safe before parsing.

github.com/dexidp/dex/server.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dexidp/dex/server to version 2.35.0 or higher.
github.com/dexidp/dex/server.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) by allowing an attacker to capture the ID token via intercepted authorization code. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by making a victim navigate to a malicious website and guiding them through the OIDC flow, stealing the OAuth authorization code in the process. The authorization code then can be exchanged by the attacker for a token, gaining access to applications accepting that token.
** Note: **
PKCE does not defend against this attack since the webserver initiates the request to Dex with a known code challenge.

github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph to version 23.0.0-rc1 or higher.
github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph.overview:
github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph is a native GraphQL database with graph backend
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Brute Force via nonce collisions in retrieving audit logs. Audit logs are AES encrypted but the nonce in use is determined by log size and is therefore predictable.

github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph/edgraph.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph/edgraph to version 21.3.0 or higher.
github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph/edgraph.overview:
github.com/dgraph-io/dgraph/edgraph is a Dgraph is a horizontally scalable and distributed GraphQL database with a graph backend.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Access Control. The /commit endpoint was not found to be protected by Access Control lists.

github.com/dhowden/tag.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dhowden/tag to version  or higher.
github.com/dhowden/tag.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) via id3v2frames.go and mp4.go.

Exploiting this vulnerability is possible by sending a crafted malicious request to the /v2/_catalog API endpoint, which results in an allocation of a massive string array and excessive use of memory.

github.com/documize/community/domain/sec.overview:
github.com/documize/community/domain/section/markdown is a markdown extention for Documize.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS). It mishandles untrusted Markdown content. This was addressed by adding the bluemonday HTML sanitizer to defend against XSS.

github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/serve.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/server to version 1.5.0 or higher.
github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/serve.overview:
github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/server is an Easy and fast file sharing from the command-line

github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/serve.overview:
github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/server is an Easy and fast file sharing from the command-line
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal that allows deletion of files.

github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/serve.remediation: Upgrade github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/server to version  or higher.
github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/serve.overview:
github.com/dutchcoders/transfer.sh/server is an Easy and fast file sharing from the command-line
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal. Given the known URL of an item on the server, you can freely read it's metadata, which includes its delete token and simply delete it. It also allows for arbitrary traversal and reading of the filesystem if the provider is local and is not using a posix-style path such as windows.
PoC
URL='http://localhost:8080/dRz9w/thing'
curl -X DELETE "$URL/$(curl $URL.metadata | jq -r .DeletionToken)"
echo "And poof. It's gone"

curl localhost:8080/AHWlO/....\private.txt

github.com/ecnepsnai/web.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ecnepsnai/web to version 1.5.2 or higher.
github.com/ecnepsnai/web.overview:
github.com/ecnepsnai/web is a Package web is a HTTP server for Golang applications.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). Websocket requests did not call AuthenticateMethod. Depending on implementation, a denial-of-service or privilege escalation vulnerability may occur in software that uses the github.com/ecnepsnai/web package with Web Sockets that have an AuthenticateMethod.
The AuthenticateMethod is not called, and UserData will be nil in request methods. Attempts to read the UserData may result in a panic. This issue only affects web sockets where an AuthenticateMethod is supplied to the handle options. Users who do not use web sockets, or users who do not require authentication are not at risk.

github.com/edgexfoundry/edgex-go.remediation: Upgrade github.com/edgexfoundry/edgex-go to version 2.0.0 or higher.
github.com/edgexfoundry/edgex-go.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass due to improper configuration settings when creating a proxy user while the EdgeX API gateway is configured for OAuth2 authentication

github.com/edgexfoundry/edgex-go/interna.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Access Control via the /api/v2/config endpoint, which allows the attacker to bypass the access controls on message bus credentials and expose the credentials when running in security-enabled mode.
Exploiting this vulnerability allows attackers to intercept data or inject fake data into the EdgeX message bus.

src.elv.sh.remediation: Upgrade src.elv.sh to version 0.14.0 or higher.
src.elv.sh.overview:
src.elv.sh is an expressive programming language and a versatile interactive shell, combined into one seamless package. It runs on Linux, BSDs, macOS and Windows.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary Code Execution. Elvish's backend for the experimental web UI (started by elvish -web) hosts an endpoint that allows executing the code sent from the web UI. The backend does not check the origin of requests correctly. As a result, if the user has the web UI backend open and visits a compromised or malicious website, the website can send arbitrary code to the endpoint in localhost.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). cmd/evm/runner.go allows attackers to cause a denial of service (SEGV) via crafted bytecode.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/cmd/geth.remediation: There is no fixed version for github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/cmd/geth.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/cmd/geth is a geth is the official command-line client for Ethereum.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). Go-Ethereum nodes crash after receiving a serial of messages and cannot be recovered. They will crash with "runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference" and give rise to a SEGV signal.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/cmd/geth.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/cmd/geth to version 1.10.9 or higher.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/cmd/geth is a geth is the official command-line client for Ethereum.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/consensu.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/consensus is a package that implements different Ethereum consensus engines.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Calculation. It could cause miners to erroneously calculate PoW in an upcoming epoch (estimated early January, 2021).  This happened on the ETC chain on 2020-11-06. 
Note: This issue is relevant only for miners, non-mining nodes are unaffected.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.remediation: There is no fixed version for github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation which allows attackers to increase rewards by mining blocks in certain situations, and using manipulation of time-difference values to achieve replacement of main-chain blocks, aka Riskless Uncle Making (RUM).

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core to version 1.10.13 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core is a package that implements the Ethereum protocol.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) by sending from a malicious node 5120 future transactions with a high gas price in one message, which can purge all of pending transactions in a victim node's memory pool.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core to version 1.10.8 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core is a package that implements the Ethereum protocol.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). A consensus-vulnerability in go-ethereum (Geth) could cause a chain split, where vulnerable versions refuse to accept the canonical chain.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core to version 1.9.20 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core is a package that implements the Ethereum protocol.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cryptographic Issues. A consensus-vulnerability could cause a chain split, where vulnerable versions refuse to accept the canonical chain.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth to version 1.9.20 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth is a package that implements the Ethereum protocol.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cryptographic Issues. A consensus-vulnerability could cause a chain split, where vulnerable versions refuse to accept the canonical chain.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core/vm.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core/vm to version 1.19.7 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core/vm.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/core/vm is a Package that implements the Ethereum Virtual Machine.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Calculation. It can be used to cause a chain-split where vulnerable nodes reject the canonical chain. Geth's pre-compiled dataCopy (at 0x00...04) contract did a shallow copy on invocation. An attacker could deploy a contract that writes X to an EVM memory region R, then calls 0x00..04 with R as an argument, then overwrites R to Y, and finally invokes the RETURNDATACOPY opcode.When this contract is invoked, a consensus-compliant node would push X on the EVM stack, whereas Geth would push Y.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth to version 1.8.14 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/eth is a package that implements the Ethereum protocol.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. TraceChain in eth/api_tracer.go does not verify that the end block is after the start block.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/les.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/les to version 1.8.11, 0.4.1 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/les.overview:
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/les is an Ethereum Go Client
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to improper validations in RPC messages, which may lead to a panic.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/les.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/les to version 1.9.25 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/les.overview:

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). A DoS vulnerability can make a LES server crash via malicious GetProofsV2 request from a connected LES client.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/miner.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/miner to version 1.9.23 or higher.

github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/p2p.remediation: Upgrade github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/p2p to version 1.10.17 or higher.
github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/p2p.overview:

Note: A possible workaround is to set the loglevel to INFO (as is default).

github.com/evergreen-ci/evergreen.remediation: Upgrade github.com/evergreen-ci/evergreen to version 0.0.0-20210928201300-30730802494d or higher.

github.com/evmos/ethermint.vul_name: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere
github.com/evmos/ethermint.remediation:
:
If it is not possible to upgrade to the fixed version, the user can redeploy the same contract, i.e. with identical bytecode, so that the original contract's code is recovered. The new contract deployment restores the bytecode hash -> bytecode entry in the internal state.
github.com/evmos/ethermint.overview:
github.com/evmos/ethermint is a scalable and interoperable Ethereum library, built on Proof-of-Stake with fast-finality using the Cosmos SDK.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere due to a bug in the DeleteAccount function, all contracts sharing identical bytecodes (i.e sharing the same CodeHash) will also stop working once one contract invokes selfdestruct, even though only a single contract invoked the selfdestruct OPCODE.

github.com/filebrowser/filebrowser/v2/ht.overview:
github.com/filebrowser/filebrowser/v2/http is a web file browser.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Request Forgery (CSRF) via a highly privileged user created by sending a malicious HTML web page to the victim, which allows access to the filesystem.

github.com/filebrowser/filebrowser/v2/http is a web file browser.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS). It allows an authenticated user to upload a malicious .svg file which acts as a stored XSS payload. If this stored XSS payload is triggered by an administrator it will trigger malicious OS commands on the server running the FileBrowser instance.

github.com/filecoin-project/lotus/chain/.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. The ffi uses blst as the signature backend, matching the existing functionality. This allows to simplify the ffi contexts, and remove the git submodule for blst.

github.com/fleetdm/fleet/v4/server/fleet.remediation: Upgrade github.com/fleetdm/fleet/v4/server/fleet to version 4.13.0 or higher.
github.com/fleetdm/fleet/v4/server/fleet.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when a team admin can erroneously add themselves as admin, maintainer, or observer on other teams.
Note:

All versions of fleet making use of the teams feature are affected by these authorization bypass issues.

Fleet instances without teams, or with teams but without restricted team accounts are not affected.

github.com/fleetdm/fleet/v4/server/servi.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when a team admin can erroneously add themselves as admin, maintainer, or observer on other teams.
Note:

All versions of fleet making use of the teams feature are affected by these authorization bypass issues.

Fleet instances without teams, or with teams but without restricted team accounts are not affected.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure by allowing to spoof SAML authentication with missing audience verification. This impacts deployments using SAML SSO in two specific cases:

A malicious or compromised Service Provider (SP) could reuse the SAML response to log into Fleet as a user -- only if the user has an account with the same email in Fleet, and the user signs into the malicious SP via SAML SSO from the same Identity Provider (IdP) configured.
A user could reuse a SAML response intended for another SP to log into Fleet. This is only a concern if the user is blocked from Fleet in the IdP, but continues to have an account in Fleet. If the user is blocked from the IdP entirely, this cannot be exploited.

Note: This vulnerability is fixed in version 4.9.1 on Github

github.com/fleetdm/fleet/v4/server/sso.remediation: Upgrade github.com/fleetdm/fleet/v4/server/sso to version 4.9.1 or higher.
github.com/fleetdm/fleet/v4/server/sso.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure by allowing to spoof SAML authentication with missing audience verification. This impacts deployments using SAML SSO in two specific cases:

A malicious or compromised Service Provider (SP) could reuse the SAML response to log into Fleet as a user -- only if the user has an account with the same email in Fleet, and the user signs into the malicious SP via SAML SSO from the same Identity Provider (IdP) configured.
A user could reuse a SAML response intended for another SP to log into Fleet. This is only a concern if the user is blocked from Fleet in the IdP, but continues to have an account in Fleet. If the user is blocked from the IdP entirely, this cannot be exploited.

Note: This vulnerability is fixed in version 4.9.1 on Github

github.com/flipped-aurora/gin-vue-admin/.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal via the download module.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal due to a lack of parameter validation, which leads to arbitrary file read.

github.com/flipped-aurora/gin-vue-admin/server/api/v1/system is a full-stack front-end and back-end separation development platform based on vue and gin development. It integrates jwt authentication, dynamic routing, dynamic menu, cabin authentication, form generator, code generator, and other functions. A variety of sample files, allows you to focus more time on business development
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass due to missing authentication validation on the setUserInfo function, low privilege users are able to modify higher privilege users.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to SQL Injection in server/service/system/sys_auto_code_pgsql.go, which means that PostgreSQL must be used as the database for this vulnerability to occur and users must require JWT login.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to SQL Injection in sys_initdb_pgsql.go file.

github.com/flipped-aurora/gin-vue-admin/server/utils is a Vue-based admin system
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary File Upload when using the plugin installation function, due to allowing a malicious attacker to upload a constructed zip package to traverse the directory and upload or overwrite arbitrary files on the server side.

github.com/flipped-aurora/gin-vue-admin/server/utils is a Vue-based admin system
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary File Upload in the makeFileContent() function in breakpoint_continue.go, which allows arbitrary files to be uploaded when accepting the fileMd5 and fileName parameters.

github.com/fluxcd/helm-controller.remediation: Upgrade github.com/fluxcd/helm-controller to version 0.19.0 or higher.
github.com/fluxcd/helm-controller.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary Code Injection via a malicious Kubeconfig. In multi-tenancy deployments, this can also lead to privilege escalation if the controller's service account has elevated permissions. 
Note: Workarounds include disabling functionality via Validating Admission webhooks by restricting users from setting the spec.kubeConfig field in Flux Kustomization and HelmRelease objects.

github.com/fluxcd/helm-controller/api/v2.overview:
github.com/fluxcd/helm-controller/api/v2beta1 is a package containing API Schema definitions for the helm v2beta1 API group +kubebuilder:object:generate=true +groupName=helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to improper handling of the metav1.Duration type in addition to a lack of validation within Flux to restrict allowed values. A user with object modification permissions can provide invalid values for fields such as .spec.interval or .spec.timeout, which are not sufficiently validated against their underlying types.

github.com/fluxcd/helm-controller/contro.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) which allows for specific data inputs to cause high memory consumption. In some platforms, this could cause the controller to panic and stop processing reconciliations. In a shared cluster multi-tenancy environment, a tenant could create a HelmRelease that makes the controller panic, denying all other tenants from their Helm releases being reconciled.

github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.remediation: Upgrade github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller to version 0.23.0 or higher.
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.overview:
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller is a Kubernetes operator, specialized in running continuous delivery pipelines for infrastructure and workloads defined with Kubernetes manifests and assembled with Kustomize.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary Code Injection via a malicious Kubeconfig. In multi-tenancy deployments, this can also lead to privilege escalation if the controller's service account has elevated permissions. 
Note: Workarounds include disabling functionality via Validating Admission webhooks by restricting users from setting the spec.kubeConfig field in Flux Kustomization and HelmRelease objects.

github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.remediation: Upgrade github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller to version 0.24.0 or higher.
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.overview:
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller is a Kubernetes operator, specialized in running continuous delivery pipelines for infrastructure and workloads defined with Kubernetes manifests and assembled with Kustomize.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Uncontrolled Recursion where a malicious user can use a specially crafted kustomization.yaml at controller level.

github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.remediation: Upgrade github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller to version 0.24.0 or higher.
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.overview:
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller is a Kubernetes operator, specialized in running continuous delivery pipelines for infrastructure and workloads defined with Kubernetes manifests and assembled with Kustomize.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal. A malicious user can use built-in features and a specially crafted kustomization.yaml to expose sensitive data from the controller’s pod filesystem. In multi-tenancy deployments this can lead to privilege escalation if the controller's service account has elevated permissions.
Within the affected versions, users with write access to a Flux source are able to use built-in features to expose sensitive data from the controller’s pod filesystem using a malicious kustomization.yaml file.

github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.remediation: Upgrade github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller to version 0.15.0 or higher.
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller.overview:
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller is a Kubernetes operator, specialized in running continuous delivery pipelines for infrastructure and workloads defined with Kubernetes manifests and assembled with Kustomize.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Command Injection. Users that can create Kubernetes Secrets, Service Accounts and Flux Kustomization objects, could execute commands inside the kustomize-controller container by embedding a shell script in a Kubernetes Secret. This can be used to run kubectl commands under the Service Account of kustomize-controller, thus allowing an authenticated Kubernetes user to gain cluster admin privileges.

github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/a.overview:
github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/api/v1beta2 is a package containing API Schema definitions for the kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io v1beta2 API group. +kubebuilder:object:generate=true +groupName=kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to improper handling of the metav1.Duration type in addition to a lack of validation within Flux to restrict allowed values. A user with object modification permissions can provide invalid values for fields such as .spec.interval or .spec.timeout, which are not sufficiently validated against their underlying types.

github.com/flynn/noise.remediation: Upgrade github.com/flynn/noise to version 1.0.0 or higher.
github.com/flynn/noise.overview:
github.com/flynn/noise is a Go implementation of the Noise Protocol Framework
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). If an attacker sends an invalid ciphertext into one peer's Decrypt, the nonce is incremented unconditionally. This causes a desync of the CipherState due to a nonce mismatch between the peers, resulting in a failure to decrypt all subsequent messages. A new handshake will be required to establish a new CipherState.

github.com/katzenpost/noise.remediation: Upgrade github.com/katzenpost/noise to version 0.0.3 or higher.
github.com/katzenpost/noise.overview:
github.com/katzenpost/noise is a Noise Protocol Framework: Katzenpost fork of the flynn noise library.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). If an attacker sends an invalid ciphertext into one peer's Decrypt, the nonce is incremented unconditionally. This causes a desync of the CipherState due to a nonce mismatch between the peers, resulting in a failure to decrypt all subsequent messages. A new handshake will be required to establish a new CipherState.

github.com/flynn/noise.remediation: Upgrade github.com/flynn/noise to version 1.0.0 or higher.
github.com/flynn/noise.overview:
github.com/flynn/noise is a Go implementation of the Noise Protocol Framework
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Integer Overflow or Wraparound. If 2^64 (~18.4 quintillion) or more messages are encrypted with Encrypt after handshaking, the nonce counter will wrap around, causing multiple messages to be encrypted with the same key and nonce, resulting in a potentially catastrophic weakening of the security properties of the symmetric cipher.

github.com/katzenpost/noise.remediation: Upgrade github.com/katzenpost/noise to version 0.0.3 or higher.
github.com/katzenpost/noise.overview:
github.com/katzenpost/noise is a Noise Protocol Framework: Katzenpost fork of the flynn noise library.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Integer Overflow or Wraparound. If 2^64 (~18.4 quintillion) or more messages are encrypted with Encrypt after handshaking, the nonce counter will wrap around, causing multiple messages to be encrypted with the same key and nonce, resulting in a potentially catastrophic weakening of the security properties of the symmetric cipher.

github.com/flyteorg/flyteadmin/auth/auth.overview:
github.com/flyteorg/flyteadmin/auth/authzserver is a gRPC + REST Service written in golang and uses a RDBMs to store meta information and management information for Flyte Tasks and Workflows.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Insufficient Session Expiration which allows authenticated users, using an external identity provider, to use access tokens and IDs even after they expired.
Note:
Users who use FlyteAdmin as the OAuth2 Authorization Server are unaffected by this issue.

github.com/flyteorg/flyteadmin/auth/conf.overview:
github.com/flyteorg/flyteadmin/auth/config is a gRPC + REST Service written in golang and uses a RDBMs to store meta information and management information for Flyte Tasks and Workflows.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Use of Hard-coded Credentials when enabling the default Flyte’s authorization server without changing the default clientid hashes.
Note:
This vulnerability only applies to users who have not specified the ExternalAuthorizationServer setting.
Using an external auth server automatically turns off this default configuration and is not susceptible to this vulnerability.

github.com/foxcpp/maddy.remediation: Upgrade github.com/foxcpp/maddy to version 0.5.1 or higher.
github.com/foxcpp/maddy.overview:
github.com/foxcpp/maddy is a Composable all-in-one mail server.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). S3 storage write is not aborted on errors leading to unbounded memory usage.

github.com/foxcpp/maddy/cmd/maddy-pam-he.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass which does not check password expiry or account expiry when authenticating using auth.pam.

github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth/pa.remediation: Upgrade github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth/pam to version 0.5.4 or higher.
github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth/pa.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass which does not check password expiry or account expiry when authenticating using auth.pam.

github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth.remediation: Upgrade github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth to version 0.6.3 or higher.
github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth.overview:
github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth is a Maddy Mail Server implements all functionality required to run a e-mail server. It can send messages via SMTP (works as MTA), accept messages via SMTP (works as MX) and store messages while providing access to them via IMAP. In addition to that it implements auxiliary protocols that are mandatory to keep email reasonably secure (DKIM, SPF, DMARC, DANE, MTA-STS).
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Authentication Bypass by Primary Weakness if the SASL authorization username is specified when using the PLAIN authentication mechanisms. Instead of validating the specified username, it is accepted as is after checking the credentials for the authentication username. 
Exploiting this vulnerability might result in full authentication bypass.

github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth/sh.overview:
github.com/foxcpp/maddy/internal/auth/shadow is a composable all-in-one mail server.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Insecure Encryption via the auth.shadow module as it uses insecure MD5 hashes.

github.com/gin-gonic/gin.remediation: There is no fixed version for github.com/gin-gonic/gin.
github.com/gin-gonic/gin.overview:
github.com/gin-gonic/gin is a package that implements a HTTP web framework called gin.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation via the FileAttachment function, due to improper handling of the filename in the Content-Disposition response header.
Exploiting this vulnerability allows the extension/file name to be tampered with when downloading files.

github.com/gin-gonic/gin.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gin-gonic/gin to version 1.9.0 or higher.
github.com/gin-gonic/gin.overview:
github.com/gin-gonic/gin is a package that implements a HTTP web framework called gin.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation by allowing an attacker to use a specially crafted request via the X-Forwarded-Prefix header, potentially leading to cache poisoning.
Note: Although this issue does not pose a significant threat on its own it can serve as an input vector for other more impactful vulnerabilities. However, successful exploitation may depend on the server configuration and whether the header is used in the application logic.

github.com/gin-gonic/gin.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gin-gonic/gin to version 1.6.0 or higher.
github.com/gin-gonic/gin.overview:
github.com/gin-gonic/gin is a package that implements a HTTP web framework called gin.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Output Neutralization for Logs such that the default Formatter for the Logger middleware (LoggerConfig.Formatter), which is included in the Default engine, allows attackers to inject arbitrary log entries by manipulating the request path.

github.com/gin-gonic/gin.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gin-gonic/gin to version 1.7.7 or higher.
github.com/gin-gonic/gin.overview:
github.com/gin-gonic/gin is a package that implements a HTTP web framework called gin.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to HTTP Response Splitting. When gin is exposed directly to the internet, a client's IP can be spoofed by setting the X-Forwarded-For header.

github.com/gin-gonic/gin.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gin-gonic/gin to version 1.6.0 or higher.
github.com/gin-gonic/gin.overview:
github.com/gin-gonic/gin is a package that implements a HTTP web framework called gin.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Log Injection due to improper sanitisation of user-controlled log output.

github.com/gin-gonic/gin.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gin-gonic/gin to version 0.5 or higher.
github.com/gin-gonic/gin.overview:
github.com/gin-gonic/gin is a package that implements a HTTP web framework called gin.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. It is possible for a client to spoof the IP address sent to a server.

github.com/github/gh-ost/go/cmd/gh-ost.remediation: Upgrade github.com/github/gh-ost/go/cmd/gh-ost to version 1.1.3 or higher.
github.com/github/gh-ost/go/cmd/gh-ost.overview:
github.com/github/gh-ost/go/cmd/gh-ost is a triggerless online schema migration solution for MySQL. It is testable and provides pausability, dynamic control/reconfiguration, auditing, and many operational perks.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Command Injection by injecting DSN strings through the -database parameter, which may lead to reading of arbitrary files.
Note: For this to be exploitable, the attacker must have access to the target host or trick an administrator into executing a malicious gh-ost command on a host running gh-ost, plus network access from host running gh-ost to the attack's malicious MySQL server.

github.com/github/git-sizer/git.remediation: Upgrade github.com/github/git-sizer/git to version 1.4.0 or higher.
github.com/github/git-sizer/git.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary Code Execution where if an attacker can get a victim to run git-sizer against a hostile repository, they can get the victim to run arbitrary code.
Note: This is only exploitable against Windows users.

aahframe.work.remediation: Upgrade aahframe.work to version 0.12.4 or higher.
aahframe.work.overview:
aahframe.work is a Go web framework.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal due to improper user-input sanitization in HTTPEngine.Handlevia, via static file delivery.

github.com/go-vela/compiler/template/nat.overview:
github.com/go-vela/compiler/template/native is a provides the ability for Vela to render a templated yaml configuration into an executable pipeline.

github.com/go-vela/server/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/go-vela/server/api to version 0.16.0 or higher.
github.com/go-vela/server/api.overview:
github.com/go-vela/server/api is a Package api provides the handlers for the Vela API.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Insecure Defaults which allows exploitation and container breakouts.
The following default configurations are impacted:

Default Privileged Images

When the target/vela-docker plugin will run as a privileged container, even if the Vela administrators did not intend to allow for any privileged plugins and even if the vela.yml configuration file does not use the privileged = True flag.
Exploiting this vulnerability allows a malicious user to easily break out of the container and access the worker host operating system.

Default Allowed Repositories

When it allows anyone with a GitHub account (or other enabled source control management solution) to enable a repository within Vela and run builds, this means that if a Vela instance is accessible to the public, a third party could add their own malicious repositories to the Vela instance and run arbitrary code.

Default Enabled Events allow Pull Requests

The default configurations allow anyone who can issue a pull request against a repository to trigger a Vela job.

github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/core/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/core/api to version 1.10.3, 2.0.1 or higher.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure. An attacker can make use of the Harbor API to make unauthenticated calls to the Harbor instance. Based on the HTTP status code in the response, an attacker is then able to work out which resources exist, and which do not in order to enumerate resources on the system.

github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/core/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/core/api to version 2.0.3, 2.1.0 or higher.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure. Non-administrator users (such as those created via self-registration) can list all usernames and user IDs by sending a GET request to /api/users/search with parameter "username" and value "_", as follows:
curl -X GET "https://<host>/api/users/search?username=_" -H "accept: application/json" --user <user>:<password>

github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/core/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/core/api to version 1.8.6, 1.9.3 or higher.

github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/core/api is a cloud native registry project that stores, signs, and scans content.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to User Enumeration.
The issue is present in the /users API endpoint. This endpoint is supposed to be restricted to administrators. This restriction is able to be bypassed and information can be obtained about registered users can be obtained via the "search" functionality.

github.com/goharbor/harbor/src/lib.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Authentication Bypass. The catalog’s registry API is exposed on an unauthenticated path. The catalog API path is served at the following path and it requires authentication as an admin:
GET /v2/_catalog
However, the authorization can be bypassed by using the following path:
GET /v2/_catalog/

GET /v2/_catalog/

github.com/gohugoio/hugo.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gohugoio/hugo to version 0.79.1 or higher.
github.com/gohugoio/hugo.overview:
github.com/gohugoio/hugo is a The world’s fastest framework for building websites.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary Code Execution. Hugo can execute a binary from the current directory on Windows. Hugo depends on Go's os/exec for certain features, e.g. for rendering of Pandoc documents if these binaries are found in the system %PATH% on Windows. However, if a malicious file with the same name (exe or bat) is found in the current working directory at the time of running hugo, the malicious command will be invoked instead of the system one.
Windows users who run hugo inside untrusted Hugo sites are affected.

github.com/google/exposure-notifications-verification-server/pkg/rbac is a Package rbac implements authorization.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Privilege Escalation. A privilege escalation exists which allows an attacker who

(1) has UserWrite permissions
(2) is using a carefully crafted request or malicious proxy

to create another user with higher privileges than their own. This occurs due to insufficient checks on the allowed set of permissions. The new user creation event would be captured in the Event Log.

github.com/google/fscrypt.remediation: A fix was pushed into the master branch but not yet published.
github.com/google/fscrypt.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Command Injection via the bash_completion script which allows injection of commands via crafted mountpoint paths, allowing privilege escalation under a specific set of circumstances. A local user who has control over mountpoint paths could potentially escalate their privileges if they create a malicious mountpoint path and if the system administrator happens to be using the fscrypt bash completion script to complete mountpoint paths.

github.com/google/fscrypt/pam.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). The PAM module for fscrypt doesn't adequately validate fscrypt metadata files, allowing users to create malicious metadata files that prevent other users from logging in. A local user can cause a denial of service by creating a fscrypt metadata file that prevents other users from logging into the system. We recommend upgrading to version 0.3.3 or above

github.com/google/fscrypt/pam.remediation: Upgrade github.com/google/fscrypt/pam to version 0.2.4 or higher.
github.com/google/fscrypt/pam.overview:

github.com/google/go-attestation/attest.overview:
github.com/google/go-attestation/attest is a Go-Attestation abstracts remote attestation operations across a variety of platforms and TPMs, enabling remote validation of machine identity and state. This project attempts to provide high-level primitives for both client and server logic.

github.com/google/go-tpm/tpm.overview:
github.com/google/go-tpm/tpm is a Go library that communicates directly with a TPM device on Linux or Windows machines.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Initialization. An improperly initialized migrationAuth value in Google's go-tpm TPM1.2 library versions prior to 0.3.0 can lead an eavesdropping attacker to discover the auth value for a key created with CreateWrapKey. An attacker listening in on the channel can collect both encUsageAuth and encMigrationAuth, and then can calculate usageAuth ^ encMigrationAuth as the migrationAuth can be guessed for all keys created with CreateWrapKey. TPM2.0 is not impacted by this. We recommend updating your library to 0.3.0 or later, or, if you cannot update, to call CreateWrapKey with a random 20-byte value for migrationAuth.

gvisor.dev/gvisor/runsc/boot/filter.remediation: A fix was pushed into the master branch but not yet published.
gvisor.dev/gvisor/runsc/boot/filter.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass. Within the seccomp sandbox, it permits access to the renameat system call, which allowed attackers to rename files on the host OS.

github.com/gopasspw/gopass/internal/tpl.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gopasspw/gopass/internal/tpl to version 1.11.0 or higher.
github.com/gopasspw/gopass/internal/tpl.overview:
github.com/gopasspw/gopass/internal/tpl is an a password manager for the command line.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Insecure Encryption. Salted hash functions used in the templates are using only 4 bytes of random salt, which can lead to them being brute-forced.

github.com/gophish/gophish.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) via a crafted payload involving autofocus.

github.com/gophish/gophish.remediation: There is no fixed version for github.com/gophish/gophish.
github.com/gophish/gophish.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS) via a crafted landing page due to improper input sanitization.

github.com/gophish/gophish.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gophish/gophish to version 0.12.0 or higher.
github.com/gophish/gophish.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. The Open Redirect vulnerability exists in the next query parameter. The application uses url.Parse(r.FormValue("next")) to extract path and eventually redirect user to a relative URL, but if next parameter starts with multiple backslashes like \\\\\\example.com, browser will redirect user to http://example.com.

github.com/gophish/gophish/controllers.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gophish/gophish/controllers to version 0.6.0 or higher.
github.com/gophish/gophish/controllers.overview:
github.com/gophish/gophish/controllers is a package middleware is responsible for the definition/​implementation of middleware functionality.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF). It is possible to access the Import Site endpoint of the Create Landing Page area without cookie/API key authentication. Unauthenticated users who can access the admin portal can use it to perform server side request forgery attacks. 
Note: this is only really an issue if the admin interface is listening on anything other than 127.0.0.1, or if it is running on 127.0.0.1 on a multiuser machine accessed by non gophish admins.

github.com/gorilla/handlers.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gorilla/handlers to version 1.3.0 or higher.
github.com/gorilla/handlers.overview:
github.com/gorilla/handlers is a collection of handlers (aka "HTTP middleware") for use with Go's net/http package (or any framework supporting http.Handler).
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Access Control. CORS is misconfigured to allow the server to reflect all origin headers. This may allow an attacker to make AJAX requests to vulnerable hosts through cross-site scripting or a malicious HTML Document, effectively bypassing the Same Origin Policy.

github.com/gotenberg/gotenberg/v7/pkg/mo.overview:
github.com/gotenberg/gotenberg/v7/pkg/modules/chromium is a Docker-powered stateless API for PDF files.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal. An SSRF vulnerability in Gotenberg exists in the remote URL to PDF conversion, which results in a remote attacker being able to read local files or fetch intranet resources.

github.com/gotenberg/gotenberg/v7/pkg/modules/chromium is a Docker-powered stateless API for PDF files.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF) via the /convert/html endpoint when the src attribute of an HTML element refers to an internal system file, such as <iframe src='file:///etc/passwd'>.

github.com/grafana/agent/cmd/agent.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/agent/cmd/agent to version 0.20.1, 0.21.2 or higher.
github.com/grafana/agent/cmd/agent.overview:
github.com/grafana/agent/cmd/agent is a Grafana Agent is a telemetry collector for sending metrics, logs, and trace data to the opinionated Grafana observability stack.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure via inline secrets defined within a metrics instance config, which are exposed in plaintext over two endpoints: metrics instance configs defined in the base YAML file are exposed at /-/config and metrics instance configs defined for the scraping service are exposed at /agent/api/v1/configs/:key. Inline secrets will be exposed to anyone being able to reach these endpoints. If HTTPS with client authentication is not configured, these endpoints are accessible to unauthenticated users. This does not apply for non-inlined secrets, such as *_file based secrets.

github.com/grafana/grafana.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana to version 8.5.22, 9.2.15, 9.3.11, 9.4.7 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS) via the FunctionDescription tooltip. This is possible due the value of the Function Description not properly sanitized. An attacker needs to have control over the Graphite data source in order to manipulate a function description and a Grafana admin needs to configure the data source, later a Grafana user needs to select a tampered function and hover over the description.

github.com/grafana/grafana.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana to version 8.2.4 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass via the fine-grained access control beta feature, which allows Grafana admins to access other users in other organizations in the instance in which they are not admins. If you cannot upgrade, you should turn off the fine-grained access control using a feature flag.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api to version 8.5.16, 9.2.8 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api to version 8.5.15, 9.2.4 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure via the "forget password" functionality on the login page. When a POST request is made to the /api/user/password/sent-reset-email API and the username or the email doesn't exist, the JSON response will contain "user not found". 
Exploiting this vulnerability allows username enumeration.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api to version 8.5.13, 9.0.9, 9.1.6 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Privilege Escalation during the migrations of translating legacy access control permissions into RBAC permissions due to a bug in the process, which grants additional access to folders or dashboards which only had Admin role.
Exploiting this vulnerability allows Editors and Viewers to edit and view the folder or the dashboards which they should have no access to.
Note:
This vulnerability affects environments where RBAC was enabled at least once and only folders/dashboards with Admin-only permissions

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel when Auth proxy authentication is used. Exploiting this vulnerability allows an escalation from Admin privileges to Server Admin when using a custom data source header with a key matching auth proxy's header.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api to version 8.3.10, 8.4.10, 8.5.9, 9.0.3 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Authentication which allows a malicious user, who has the authorization to log in via an OAuth integration that provides a login name, to take over the account of another user in the same Grafana instance.
Note: This is exploitable only if:

the malicious user is authorized to log in to Grafana via OAuth

the malicious user's external user id is not already associated with an account in Grafana

the malicious user's email address is not already associated with an account in Grafana

the malicious user knows the Grafana username or email of the target user

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api to version 7.5.15, 8.3.5 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure. This vulnerability only impacts the following API endpoints:
/teams/:teamId - an authenticated attacker can view unintended data by querying for the specific team ID.
/teams/:search - an authenticated attacker can search for teams and see the total number of available teams, including for those teams that the user does not have access to.
/teams/:teamId/members - when editors_can_admin flag is enabled, an authenticated attacker can see unintended data by querying for the specific team ID.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api to version 7.5.12, 8.3.2 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal via fully lowercase or fully uppercase .md files. Authenticated users can access .md files using this vulnerability.
The exact vulnerable URL path is /api/plugins/.*/markdown/.* for .md files.
A possible workaround is running a reverse proxy in front of Grafana that normalizes the PATH of the request. Another way is to block "/api/plugins/.*/markdown/.*" for fully lowercase or fully uppercase .md files.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is an open and composable observability and data visualization platform.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Authentication Bypass. Both unauthenticated and authenticated users are able to view the snapshot with the lowest database key by accessing the literal paths /dashboard/snapshot/:key or /api/snapshots/:key.
Additionally, if the snapshot public_mode configuration setting is set to true (the default is false), unauthenticated users are also able to delete the snapshot with the lowest database key by accessing the literal path /api/snapshots-delete/:deleteKey. Authenticated users are able to delete the snapshot with the lowest database key regardless of the snapshot public_mode, by accessing either one of the literal paths: /api/snapshots/:key or /api/snapshots-delete/:deleteKey.
The combination of deletion and viewing enables a complete walk through all snapshot data while resulting in complete snapshot data loss.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api to version 4.6.4, 5.2.3 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api is a tool for monitoring and metric analytics & dashboards for Graphite, InfluxDB & Prometheus & More.
Affected versions of the package are vulnerable to Authentication Bypass. An attacker could generate a valid cookie knowing only a username of an LDAP or OAuth user.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services to version 4.6.4, 5.2.3 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services is a tool for monitoring and metric analytics & dashboards for Graphite, InfluxDB & Prometheus & More.
Affected versions of the package are vulnerable to Authentication Bypass. An attacker could generate a valid cookie knowing only a username of an LDAP or OAuth user.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api/avata.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api/avatar to version 6.7.4, 7.0.2 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api/avata.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api/avatar is a tool for beautiful monitoring and metric analytics & dashboards for Graphite, InfluxDB & Prometheus & More.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Access Control. It is possible for an unauthenticated user/client to make Grafana send HTTP requests to any URL and return its result to the user/client. This can be used to gain information about the network that Grafana is running on, leading to a Server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability, due to insufficient access control.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api/plugi.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/api/pluginproxy is an open-source platform for monitoring and observability.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS) due to missing sanitization, which makes it possible for an attacker to serve HTML content through the Grafana datasource or plugin proxy in order to trick a user to visit this HTML page using a specially crafted link.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/middlewar.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/middleware to version 7.4.2 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/middlewar.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/middleware is a The open-source platform for monitoring and observability.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). The snapshot feature in Grafana can allow an unauthenticated remote attackers to trigger a Denial of Service via a remote API call if a commonly used configuration is set.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/plugins.remediation: A fix was pushed into the master branch but not yet published.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/plugins.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via the renderLegendFormat function, which uses a regular expression that takes polynomial time to match.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/plugins/m.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature by allowing an attacker to convince a server admin to download and successfully run a malicious plugin even though unsigned plugins are not allowed.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services to version 9.2.17, 9.3.19, 9.4.9 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure such that by enabling the url_login configuration option (disabled by default), a JWT might be sent to data sources. If an attacker has access to the data source, the leaked token could be used to authenticate to Grafana.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services/.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure. In affected versions when a data source has the Forward OAuth Identity feature enabled, sending a query to that datasource with an API token (and no other user credentials) will forward the OAuth Identity of the most recently logged-in user. This can allow API token holders to retrieve data for which they may not have intended access.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Authentication by allowing one user to block another user's login attempt by registering someone else'e email address as a username, due to Grafana allowing users to log in with either their username or email address.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb to version 8.3.2 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb is a developer testing tool for Grafana.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Directory Traversal which allows authenticated users access to arbitrary .csv files. This is relevant only if the Grafana instances have the TestData DB data source tool enabled and configured.
A possible workaround is running a reverse proxy in front of Grafana that normalizes the PATH of the request will mitigate the vulnerability.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb/mssq.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb/mssql to version 8.0.0-beta1 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb/mssq.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure. Grafana allows any user to add data sources by the source host and port. However, when Grafana connects to these data sources, it reveals the host and port as part of the error response messages, as well as other information from which one an attacker could infer the state of the host and port (port is open, closed, http, non-http, etc.).

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb/sqle.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure. Grafana allows any user to add data sources by the source host and port. However, when Grafana connects to these data sources, it reveals the host and port as part of the error response messages, as well as other information from which one an attacker could infer the state of the host and port (port is open, closed, http, non-http, etc.).

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb/mysq.overview:
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/tsdb/mysql is a tool for beautiful monitoring and metric analytics & dashboards for Graphite, InfluxDB & Prometheus & More.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Arbitrary File Read via MySQL data source. An authenticated attacker that has privileges can modify the data source configurations.

github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/web.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/web to version 9.2.4 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/web.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition in the HTTP context creation, which results in an HTTP request being assigned the authentication/authorization middleware of another call. Under heavy load, a call protected by a privileged middleware may receive the middleware of a public query instead.
Exploiting this vulnerability allows an unauthenticated user to successfully query protected endpoints.

github.com/grafana/grafana/public/app.remediation: Upgrade github.com/grafana/grafana/public/app to version 8.5.16, 9.2.10, 9.3.6 or higher.
github.com/grafana/grafana/public/app.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS) via the core plugin GeoMap, due to SVG-files improper sanitization. Exploiting this vulnerability allowed arbitrary JavaScript to be executed in the context of the currently authorized user of the Grafana instance.

github.com/graph-gophers/graphql-go/inte.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). An attacker with specifically designed queries can cause stack overflow panics. Any user with access to the GraphQL handler can send these queries and cause stack overflows. This could potentially compromise the ability of the server to serve data to its users.

github.com/gravitational/teleport.remediation: Upgrade github.com/gravitational/teleport to version 4.3.10, 4.4.8, 5.2.1 or higher.
github.com/gravitational/teleport.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) via a OOM due to unchecked size in ReadJSON.

github.com/gravitational/teleport/api/ty.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Certificate Validation. An attacker in possession of a valid Trusted Cluster join token could inject a malicious CA into a Teleport cluster that would allow them to bypass root cluster authorization and potentially connect to any node within the root cluster.
Note: Fixed versions are available on Github. Please upgrade to version 4.4.12, 5.2.5, 6.2.31, 7.3.17, 8.3.3 or higher.

github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/au.overview:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Certificate Validation. An attacker in possession of a valid Trusted Cluster join token could inject a malicious CA into a Teleport cluster that would allow them to bypass root cluster authorization and potentially connect to any node within the root cluster.
Note: Fixed versions are available on Github. Please upgrade to version 4.4.12, 5.2.5, 6.2.31, 7.3.17, 8.3.3 or higher.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Command Injection via a crafted ssh-agent installation link, by URL encoding a bash escape with carriage return line feed which can be used in place of a token and sent to a user.

This vulnerability is fixed in versions 7.1.1, 6.2.12, 5.2.4 and 4.4.11 which are available on Github.

This vulnerability is fixed in versions 7.1.1, 6.2.12, 5.2.4 and 4.4.11 which are available on Github.

github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/cl.overview:

github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/client is a privileged access management tool.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Validation. Host certificate validation was disabled for clusters where connections were terminated at the node, giving an attacker the ability to impersonate a Teleport node without detection when connecting through the Web UI.

github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/sr.overview:

github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/ut.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Privilege Escalation when setting up agent forwarding. This allows users to grant arbitrary file permissions to themselves.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). The parsing code runtime grows exponentially with nested selectors (e.g. {{a.b.c.d.e.f}}), mostly due to memory churn from slice allocations. If an attacker can submit these expressions for parsing, they can DoS the auth server with relatively small payloads (<1MB).

github.com/gravitl/netmaker/controllers.overview: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass which makes it possible for non-privileged users to run privileged API calls. Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Access Control. If a specific ACL rule is used for prefix matching in a policy, keys not matching that specific prefix can be deleted by a token using that policy with default deny settings configured.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent to version 1.6.6, 1.7.4 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). The package included an HTTP API (introduced in 1.2.0) and DNS (introduced in 1.4.3) caching feature that was vulnerable to denial of service.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/config.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/config to version 1.6.6, 1.7.4 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/config.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/config is a package, part of Hashicorps, consul.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS). The package included an HTTP API (introduced in 1.2.0) and DNS (introduced in 1.4.3) caching feature that was vulnerable to denial of service.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent to version 1.6.6, 1.7.4 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Privilege Escalation. Consul has two types of tokens, Global and Local. Local tokens are meant to only be resolvable and used within a single datacenter. Creation of local tokens is only allowed if either token replication is enabled in a secondary datacenter, or if the datacenter the token is scoped to is the primary. In a typical cluster we expect that either token replication is on in all datacenters or local tokens are not used at all.
When token replication is not enabled in a secondary datacenter, attempts to use a local token created in the primary are successful for operations targeting that secondary datacenter. Thus what was meant to be scoped to a single datacenter is valid in other datacenters.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/struct.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/structs to version 1.6.6, 1.7.4 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/struct.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/structs is a tool for service discovery and configuration.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Privilege Escalation. Consul has two types of tokens, Global and Local. Local tokens are meant to only be resolvable and used within a single datacenter. Creation of local tokens is only allowed if either token replication is enabled in a secondary datacenter, or if the datacenter the token is scoped to is the primary. In a typical cluster we expect that either token replication is on in all datacenters or local tokens are not used at all.
When token replication is not enabled in a secondary datacenter, attempts to use a local token created in the primary are successful for operations targeting that secondary datacenter. Thus what was meant to be scoped to a single datacenter is valid in other datacenters.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation in the auto-config JWT authorization checks. It allows malicious actors to construct requests which incorrectly pass custom JWT claim validation for the AutoConfig.InitialConfiguration endpoint.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul to version 1.10.2, 1.9.9, 1.8.15 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Authorization. The Txn.Apply endpoint allowed anyone with service:write permissions for service could register proxies for any other services, enabling access to traffic to the other services.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul to version 1.10.2, 1.9.9, 1.8.15 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Access Control. Raft Requests were found to not be authorized. This allowed non-server agents with a valid certificate signed by the same CA to access server-only functionality.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul to version 1.8.6 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Privilege Escalation. Allows operators with operator:read ACL permissions to read the Connect CA private key configuration when explicitly configured with the /v1/connect/ca/configuration endpoint, including the private key. This allows the user to effectively privilege escalate by enabling the ability to mint certificates for any Consul Connect services. This would potentially allow them to masquerade (receive/send traffic) as any service in the mesh.

github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.remediation: Upgrade github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul to version 1.8.5 or higher.
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul.overview:
github.com/hashicorp/consul/agent/consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) via Consul Enterprise Namespace Config Entry Replication. An operator with service:write ACL permissions in a Consul Enterprise cluster could write a malicious config entry that caused infinite raft writes due to issues with the namespace replication logic.
